items.question: For logits z = [2, 1, 0], what property must softmax output π satisfy?
options: Each π_k ≥ 0 and max π_k = 1 always
π equals z normalized by L2 norm
items.explanation: Softmax maps to the interior of the probability simplex: strictly positive entries summing to 1.

items.question: Logits z = [1000, 999, 998]. Why subtract max(z) before exp?
options: It is an approximation for speed
It prevents exp overflow while preserving exact softmax
It makes the largest class probability exactly 1
It converts logits to one-hot labels
items.answer: It prevents exp overflow while preserving exact softmax
items.explanation: Shift-invariance: softmax(z) = softmax(z − m). With m = max(z), all exponents are ≤ 0.

items.question: Logits [2.0, 1.0, 0.5], true class index 0. Approximate loss −log π_0?
Which input should nn.CrossEntropyLoss receive?
items.explanation: CrossEntropyLoss expects unnormalized logits and applies log_softmax internally.

items.question: Three independent product tags (cat, sale, new). Best output activation?
items.explanation: Independent multi-label labels need Bernoulli sigmoid per tag, not mutually exclusive softmax.
